evals.prompt:
running /vibe. develop just finished. TaskList shows the epic has 4 children: 3 completed, 1 still in_progress (worker crashed mid-impl). git diff --stat shows changes from all 4 including partial work from the crashed one.

what happens next?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the develop stage is incomplete because not all children completed. Reports per-child status (which succeeded, which failed). Does NOT proceed to simplify/review/commit. Suggests /vibe --continue or /develop to retry.
expectations: Checks child task statuses — does not assume develop succeeded just because it returned
Does NOT proceed to simplify or commit when a child task is still in_progress with partial work
Reports which tasks succeeded vs failed and suggests recovery (/vibe --continue or /develop)

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add user avatars'. branch created, scope completed (vibe_stage='scope', scope task approved with metadata.design populated). invoked Skill('develop') but it errors out completely — skill call fails, zero progress.

show error handling.
evals.expected_output: Keeps vibe_stage at 'scope' (not 'develop') since the stage failed with no progress. Leaves tracker in_progress for --continue recovery. Reports completed vs failed stages and suggests /vibe --continue or /develop.
expectations: Does NOT update vibe_stage to 'develop' — stays at 'scope' because develop made no progress
Leaves tracker in_progress (not completed/failed) so --continue can find it
Reports which stages completed vs failed and suggests /vibe --continue or /develop

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add webhook retry with exponential backoff'. branch created, about to invoke scope.

show the exact Skill() call for scope and explain how it differs from a user invoking /scope directly.
evals.expected_output: Calls Skill('scope', args='add webhook retry with exponential backoff --no-develop --auto'). Explains --auto skips both spec review and plan review gates — auto-approving instead of halting for user feedback. Scope produces metadata.spec and metadata.design with status_detail='approved'.
expectations: Skill call includes --auto flag: Skill('scope', args='<prompt> --no-develop --auto')
Explains --auto skips BOTH spec and plan review gates
Scope produces metadata.spec and metadata.design with status_detail='approved'

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add search feature'. just created the vibe tracker (task #55). about to do the Branch stage. I'm on main.

show the exact steps and tool calls for creating the branch.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT call Skill('start'). Instead inlines branch creation: generates slug via ct tool, calls Skill(gt:gt, 'create luan/<slug>'), links tracker via TaskUpdate. Then immediately continues to Scope without stopping.
expectations: Does NOT call Skill('start') — inlines branch creation instead
Uses Skill(gt:gt, 'create <username>/<slug>') for branch creation
After branch creation, immediately continues to Scope — does not end response or pause

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add auth passthrough for tests'. branch just created via Skill(gt:gt). confirmed on new branch. updated vibe_stage to 'branch'.

what do you do immediately?
evals.expected_output: Outputs the stage announcement (e.g. '[2/6] Scope') then immediately invokes Skill('scope', args='<prompt> --no-develop --auto'). Does not pause, summarize, or report branch status.
expectations: Immediately invokes Skill('scope', args='<prompt> --no-develop --auto') without pausing or waiting
Outputs stage announcement (e.g. '[2/6] Scope') as text BEFORE the Skill() call
Does not output branch creation summary or status report before continuing

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add dark mode toggle'. branch done, scope done (vibe_stage='scope'). scope task has status_detail='approved' and metadata.design populated. --dry-run was NOT passed.

just updated vibe_stage to 'scope'. what's next?
evals.expected_output: Outputs '[3/6] Develop' then immediately invokes Skill('develop'). No scope summary, no user prompt, no pause.
expectations: Immediately invokes Skill('develop') — no pause, scope summary, or user prompt
Outputs stage announcement (e.g. '[3/6] Develop') BEFORE invoking Skill('develop')
Does NOT output scope results, phase breakdowns, or any summary of what scope produced

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'add rate limiting middleware'. all prior stages done (branch, scope, develop). all epic children completed. git diff shows changes across 5 files.

just updated vibe_stage to 'develop'. what happens next?
evals.expected_output: Invokes Skill('simplify'), then Skill('review'), then Skill('commit') — all three in sequence without pausing between them.
expectations: Immediately invokes Skill('simplify') — does not skip to review or commit
After simplify, invokes Skill('review') before commit
Does not pause, summarize, or wait for user input between develop -> simplify -> review -> commit

evals.prompt:
about to run /vibe 'add search feature'. no flags. on the main branch.

list all stages in order with their [N/M] numbers.
evals.expected_output: M = 6 for full pipeline. Stages: [1/6] Branch, [2/6] Scope, [3/6] Develop, [4/6] Simplify, [5/6] Review, [6/6] Commit.
expectations: M = 6 for the full pipeline: branch, scope, develop, simplify, review, commit
Stages ordered: [1/6] Branch, [2/6] Scope, [3/6] Develop, [4/6] Simplify, [5/6] Review, [6/6] Commit

evals.expected_output: Resumes Task 2 only (session_id matches sess-AAA). Invokes /vibe --continue (not /super-vibe) since Task 2 is a vibe tracker. Does not touch Task 1 or Task 3 — they belong to other sessions.
expectations: Resumes Task 2 (session_id matches sess-AAA) — ignores Task 1 and Task 3
Invokes /vibe --continue (not /supervibe) because Task 2 has no super_vibe metadata
Does not attempt to resume, modify, or report on Task 1 or Task 3

evals.prompt:
running /vibe 'implement retry logic for failed API calls'. on main branch. no flags passed. the tracker task is already created (task #72, vibe_stage='started').

walk me through the complete Branch stage — every tool call, in order. then show what happens immediately after.
evals.expected_output: Branch stage: 1) ct tool slug to generate slug, 2) Skill(gt:gt, 'create luan/<slug>'), 3) TaskUpdate to link branch. Does NOT use Skill('start'). Immediately after: updates vibe_stage to 'branch', outputs '[2/6] Scope', invokes Skill('scope', args='implement retry logic for failed API calls --no-develop --auto'). No pause between branch and scope.
expectations: Branch creation uses inline steps (ct tool slug + Skill(gt:gt)) — NOT Skill('start')
After branch, immediately outputs '[2/6] Scope' and invokes Skill('scope') in the same response
Does not end response, pause, or output a branch status summary between Branch and Scope
